paper_title: Paleoenvironmental Interpretation of the Strelley Pool Formation

methods.description: This method involves the petrographic analysis of oriented thin sections to determine the proportions of sand, silt, clay, and different minerals. It is used to understand the mineralogical composition and textural features of the rock samples.

steps.description: A Swift automated stage linked to a Hacker electronic counting box were used to point count oriented thin sections prepared vertical to bedding for proportions of sand, silt, clay, and different mineral.
steps.searchable_summary: perform point counting on thin sections to determine mineral proportions
outputs: proportions of sand, silt, clay, and minerals

steps.description: Grains 5 μm in size and smaller were counted as clay, rather 2 μm, because pervasive silicification and recrystallization produced crystallites no less than 5 μm across.
steps.searchable_summary: classify grains 5 μm and smaller as clay

steps.description: Two separate 500-point counts were made, one for grain size, and another for mineral composition of each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct 500-point counts for grain size and mineral composition

methods.description: This method involves chemical analyses of selected samples using XRF and Pratt titration to determine the chemical composition and calculate mass transfer, strain, concentration factors, and chemical index of alteration.

steps.description: Chemical analyses of selected samples were by ALS Chemex of Vancouver, British Columbia, using XRF on glass discs, and Pratt titration for FeO.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRF and Pratt titration for chemical analysis

steps.description: The standard for the analyses was Canadian Certified Reference Materials Project standard SY-4, diorite gneiss from near Bancroft, Ontario, and 89 replicate analyses of the standard were used to calculate errors (2σ).
steps.searchable_summary: use standard SY-4 for error calculation in chemical analysis

steps.description: Bulk densities were determined from 20-40 g samples by weighing raw and after paraffin coating, in and out of water.
steps.searchable_summary: determine bulk densities using paraffin coating method

steps.description: These data were used to calculate mass transfer and strain, concentration factors, and chemical index of alteration.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mass transfer, strain, concentration factors, and chemical index of alteration

methods.description: This method involves the separation and annealing of zircon crystals for U-Pb age determination to date geological samples.

steps.description: Small (100-200 μm), equant to prismatic, euhedral to subhedral zircon crystals were separated by density and magnetism in the Isotope Geology Laboratory of Boise State University by magnetic drum separation.
steps.searchable_summary: separate zircon crystals using density and magnetic drum separation

steps.description: The zircon separate was placed in a muffle furnace at 900˚C for 60 hours in quartz beakers in order to anneal minor radiation damage.
steps.searchable_summary: anneal zircon crystals in muffle furnace at 900˚C
